"  
Sec. 1  
[The equation involves a fourth power of x and an absolute value term, which suggests symmetry about the y-axis. Since the absolute value function is even, the behavior of the equation for positive x will mirror that for negative x. This symmetry implies that any solution for positive x will have a corresponding solution for negative x. However, we must consider the possibility of a solution at x = 0 separately.]

Sec. 2  
[To determine the number of real solutions, we can analyze the function f(x) = (1/3)x^4 + 5|x| - 7. The function is continuous and differentiable everywhere except possibly at x = 0. By evaluating the function at key points and considering its behavior as x approaches infinity, we can determine how many times the function crosses the x-axis. The equation will have a certain number of real solutions based on these observations.]
